hard.next_step: 3/10/26 RH Demo with Damiano Grillo. ANA stood out as key differentiator. Intake is less important as tool will be used by sourcing team. 3/06/26 RH Submitted RFI. Offered to provide a value engineering study to promote the business case.
hard.ais_why: Damiano Grillo asked about autonomous negotiation agent capabilities and AI-generated RFP questions across the 03 Feb and 11 Mar calls, mapping to Merlin ANA, but tempered by his own words that New Balance is far from autonomous sourcing today.

north_star_verdict.headline: The 11 Mar sourcing demo landed well, but Damiano Grillo took the findings to New Balance leadership and has gone silent for nearly four months, with no RFI feedback, no named budget owner, and no forward step despite 165 days to close.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 03 Feb 2026, entered Formal Evaluation 21 May 2026 (rep-side stage move). Last genuine buyer touch 11 Mar 2026 demo, roughly 117 days ago as of 06 Jul. The only activity since is rep-side 'Call Attempted' health tasks on 12 May and internal stage/forecast edits, not buyer engagement. Close date 18 Dec 2026 is 165 days out. A Formal Evaluation this early normally moves demo to RFI-feedback to shortlist in 60 to 90 days; this one stalled after the demo, so the date is not a schedule, it is a placeholder.
evidence: Last real buyer exchange: 11 Mar 2026 demo workshop with Damiano Grillo; nothing buyer-side since.

Correcting prior memory: Zycus DID submit the RFI response on 06 Mar and Damiano acknowledged it on 09 Mar ('Thank you for your submission, I will be in touch') before the 11 Mar demo, so the RFI is delivered, not outstanding.

On 11 Mar Damiano closed with 'I'll definitely pass it to leadership the findings of this demo, and that's with them to evaluate, and I'll be in touch with any next steps or feedback' - that feedback never came.
No named economic buyer or decision maker; the evaluation was a request 'that came down from procurement leadership' and Damiano is the only contact.
Buyer repeatedly framed the effort as early and exploratory: 'this is a very early stage evaluation' and 'we're far away from that' on autonomous capabilities.
Pipeline forecast on a deal dark ~117 days with no business case funded overstates the number; Omitted is the honest category until Damiano re-engages.

metrics.narrative: The pain is confirmed but never quantified. On the 03 Feb discovery call Damiano Grillo (Indirect Category Manager, New Balance) said the biggest problems are internal and supplier ease of use and that with Ariba 'we're just utilizing Ariba to send out an RFP and collect information, then we're doing a lot of the work outside the tool', pointing to manual offline negotiation. On the 11 Mar demo he tied the strain to global headcount growth: 'as the team is getting larger across the globe, having a solution that allows us to onboard associates and get them off the ground relatively quickly is imperative.' When Rick Hunsinger asked directly whether the impact was quantitative, Damiano answered 'it's tough to say quantitative right now.' No cost-per-event, cycle-time, savings, or onboarding-time figure exists in any call or field. Converting this qualitative pain into a numbers-based value case is the gating step to reach leadership, and Rick already offered a value-engineering study (Next_Step__c, 06 Mar) to do exactly that.
sources: Avoma discovery call 03 Feb 2026, Damiano Grillo
Avoma demo workshop 11 Mar 2026, Damiano Grillo

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named, but the calls reveal where budget authority sits. On 11 Mar Damiano said the initiative 'will require a business case' and that the whole evaluation 'was a request that came down from procurement leadership to say, well, what's really out there.' On 03 Feb he described the reporting line: two other sourcing managers and himself report into a 'director of global sourcing for indirect.' That global indirect sourcing director (unnamed in Salesforce) is the most likely budget owner and sponsor of the exploration. No OpportunityContactRole, task, or MEDDPICC field names this person, so they cannot be asserted as a named stakeholder. Mapping and reaching the global indirect sourcing director, with a business case Damiano can carry up, is the single highest-value gap to close.
sources: Avoma discovery call 03 Feb 2026, Damiano Grillo
Avoma demo workshop 11 Mar 2026, Damiano Grillo

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are clear from the buyer's own words even though no scorecard is on record. Damiano's stated criteria across both calls are: internal ease of use for a growing team, supplier-side ease of use to reduce vendor friction, advanced and autonomous negotiation capability (he referenced Jaggaer's expressive-feedback and ASO optimizer as the bar), scalability to onboard associates quickly across EMEA and APAC, and flexible taxonomy that respects New Balance's own schema ('New Balance kinda has their own definitions of schema... what Ariba defines things is one of the biggest challenges we have'). Best-in-class fit for the sourcing module is the frame: 'is this what we have best in class? Is it not?' These are prospect-confirmed criteria; what is not yet defined is a formal weighted RFP scoring model, which would surface only if leadership funds the next round.
sources: Avoma discovery call 03 Feb 2026, Damiano Grillo
Avoma demo workshop 11 Mar 2026, Damiano Grillo

decision_process.narrative: The process the buyer is actually running is a leadership-sponsored market scan, read directly from the call sequence: procurement leadership asked Damiano to survey the sourcing-tool market, Zycus responded to a formal RFI (submitted 06 Mar, acknowledged by Damiano 09 Mar), and delivered a walkthrough demo on 11 Mar. Damiano's own close describes the next step: 'I'll pass it to leadership the findings of this demo, and that's with them to evaluate.' So the process gate is an internal leadership review of RFI plus demo findings, followed by a business case, before any shortlist or budget decision. The chain has stalled at that internal review with no visibility for nearly four months. No formal timeline, no committee, and no decision date have been shared, which is itself the risk: the process is entirely inside New Balance and single-threaded through Damiano.
sources: Avoma demo workshop 11 Mar 2026, Damiano Grillo
SF activity 09 Mar 2026 (Clari email, Damiano Grillo acknowledging RFI)

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics exist yet, which is normal for an early Formal Evaluation that has not reached shortlist or commercials. There is no MSA, SOW, order form, pricing, legal, or InfoSec activity in any field or call. The one adjacent signal is that at New Balance 'contract management lives with the legal group as of today' (Damiano, 03 Feb), so legal is a separate function the deal has not yet touched. Paper process will only become visible after leadership funds a real evaluation and a commercial round begins; the move that would create that visibility is securing the business case and an executive sponsor, not chasing paperwork now.
sources: Avoma discovery call 03 Feb 2026, Damiano Grillo

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is well articulated and consistent across both calls, owned by Damiano and his indirect sourcing team. Core pain: Ariba's sourcing module is hard to use internally and for suppliers ('utilizing our sourcing tool today becomes a bit of a pain point and causes friction with our supplier base'), the team does 'a lot of the work outside the tool', negotiations are still 'traditional face to face, potentially dabbling in reverse auctions', and provider support is weak. A second, forward-looking pain is scale: the team is growing across EMEA and APAC and needs a tool that onboards new associates fast. A third is spend-analytics inflexibility, where Ariba cannot match New Balance's custom category schema. What keeps this at confirmed-pain rather than compelling-event is that Damiano twice stressed the org is 'far away' from autonomous sourcing and needs internal maturity and change management first, so the pain is real but not yet urgent enough to force a purchase.
sources: Avoma discovery call 03 Feb 2026, Damiano Grillo
Avoma demo workshop 11 Mar 2026, Damiano Grillo

champion.narrative: Damiano Grillo (Indirect Category Manager) is a developing champion who has cooled. What developed: he initiated the engagement, sat a formal RFI, drove a substantive 11 Mar demo asking sharp questions on autonomous negotiation, Excel bid-sheet upload, multi-round events, and taxonomy flexibility, and gave positive read-outs ('definitely looks easier to use and more robust than what we have today'). What is missing: he has introduced no other stakeholder, has not shared a draft RFI or internal document, controls no budget, and explicitly defers the decision to leadership ('that's with them to evaluate'). Most critically he has been silent since 11 Mar, roughly 117 days, and never returned the feedback he promised. He is a genuine coach but not yet a mobilizing champion; his access to the budget owner is unconfirmed. Re-engaging him, diagnosing the silence, and co-building the business case so he can champion upward is the path to strengthen this.
sources: Avoma discovery call 03 Feb 2026, Damiano Grillo
Avoma demo workshop 11 Mar 2026, Damiano Grillo
SF LastActivityDate 11 Mar 2026 (no buyer touch since)

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the incumbent, but the real fight is against status-quo inertia, not a rival vendor. New Balance runs Ariba across P2P (a committed, entrenched global rollout Damiano is explicit about NOT replacing: 'that is a long term commitment on that side') and for the eRFX sourcing module plus spend analytics, and only sourcing and analytics are in play. Damiano's dissatisfaction with Ariba sourcing is clear (ease of use, supplier friction, weak support, inflexible schema, no advanced negotiation), yet he framed the whole effort as 'a very early stage evaluation' driven by curiosity from procurement leadership, and repeatedly said the org is 'far away' and needs internal maturity. Jaggaer surfaces only as Damiano's positive prior-life reference for autonomous sourcing (a benchmark, not an active bidder here). No other vendor (Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, Zip, Keelvar) was named. The wedge over both Ariba and do-nothing is Merlin autonomous negotiation for tail spend plus ease-of-use and flexible taxonomy; the way we win is to make the sourcing-only value undeniable and quantified so leadership funds a move rather than defaulting to the incumbent.
sources: Avoma discovery call 03 Feb 2026, Damiano Grillo
Avoma demo workshop 11 Mar 2026, Damiano Grillo
SF Next_Step__c 10 Mar 2026 (ANA stood out as key differentiator)

critical_signals.text: The 11 Mar sourcing and spend-analytics demo landed well, with Damiano calling it easier to use and more robust than Ariba, yet he handed the findings to leadership and has not responded since.
Ariba stays committed on P2P and only the sourcing module is contestable, so the threat is New Balance keeping the status quo and doing nothing, not another vendor.
A business case is the stated gate to advance, but Damiano said the impact is tough to quantify right now and no budget or budget owner is confirmed.
Procurement leadership sponsored the exploration and a global indirect sourcing director owns the team, but neither has been met and the deal rides entirely on Damiano.

deal_movement.summary: Two substantive calls in Feb and Mar built real interest, then the deal went dark. Correcting the prior record: the RFI response was submitted (06 Mar) and acknowledged by Damiano (09 Mar), and the demo was delivered (11 Mar); it did not fail to happen. Since 11 Mar the only movement is rep-side (health-check call attempts on 12 May, internal stage move to Formal Evaluation on 21 May, forecast edits), not buyer engagement. The ball is with Damiano and New Balance leadership to return demo feedback.

Damiano acknowledged the RFI submission and confirmed the upcoming demo workshop
Demo workshop delivered; ANA and spend analytics well received, Damiano to take findings to leadership
Rep logged 'Call Attempted' opportunity-health tasks flagging the prospect had gone cold and no decision maker mapped
Internal stage moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation (rep-side, not a buyer event)

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the incumbent, entrenched on P2P (committed rollout, not being replaced) and under evaluation only for the eRFX sourcing module and spend analytics. The single strongest CURRENT threat is not a rival vendor but status-quo do-nothing: as of 11 Mar 2026 Damiano called this 'a very early stage evaluation' and said New Balance is 'far away' from acting, with no business case funded and no decision date. Jaggaer appears only as Damiano's favourable prior-role reference for autonomous sourcing, a benchmark rather than an active competitor. No other vendor is named in any source.

competitors.source: Avoma demo workshop 11 Mar 2026, Damiano Grillo
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the sourcing-only value (negotiation savings on tail spend, onboarding-time and supplier-friction reduction) into a leadership-ready business case, and make the Dec close a forcing function for FY2027 planning.

competitors.quote: We utilize Ariba today, and we're looking to evaluate what other platforms can do for that space... we're just utilizing Ariba to send out an RFP and collect information, then we're doing a lot of the work outside the tool.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 03 Feb 2026 and demo 11 Mar 2026, Damiano Grillo
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin autonomous negotiation and drag-and-drop ease of use that Ariba's eRFX lacks, plus flexible taxonomy that respects New Balance's own schema; keep scope to sourcing so the P2P commitment is not a battle.

competitors.name: Jaggaer
competitors.quote: I know back when I was utilizing Jaggaer, they had expressive feedback capabilities, which was super impactful for our negotiations and drove a lot of value.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 03 Feb 2026, Damiano Grillo
competitors.how_we_win: Reference is Damiano's prior-life experience, not an active bidder; match the expressive-feedback and optimizer capability he valued with Merlin ANA so Zycus becomes the successor, not the challenger.

best_practice_check.summary: This is an early, dark, single-threaded deal where the buyer's own gate is a business case that reaches leadership; the win-strategy levers all point at restarting motion around the narrow sourcing scope.
flags: Break the single thread: map and reach the global indirect sourcing director who owns the team and budget, because the entire deal currently rests on one category manager who has gone quiet.
Convert Damiano's qualitative pain into a quantified, leadership-ready value case (tail-spend savings, onboarding-time and supplier-friction reduction), the exact gate he named for advancing.

Anchor on the sourcing-plus-ANA scope and name the do-nothing risk directly: make Merlin autonomous negotiation and flexible taxonomy the undeniable wedge Ariba cannot match, so status quo becomes the costly option.

AI is a differentiator here, not yet a driver.

scope_change.detail: Scope has been narrow and sourcing-focused since creation; buyer explicitly excludes P2P, intake, and contract management. Amount rose 100k to 250k on 04 Feb, no reduction.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A strong 11 Mar sourcing demo won Damiano's interest, but the deal is now dark for nearly four months with no funded business case, no named budget owner, and the org itself saying it is far from ready, so the real contest is against status-quo inertia, not a rival.

win_position.text: This sits weak: the only engaged contact, category manager Damiano Grillo, went silent after the 11 Mar demo and the evaluation is a leadership curiosity request with no budget behind it.
Product fit is genuinely strong: Damiano called Zycus sourcing and spend analytics easier to use and more robust than Ariba and was intrigued by Merlin autonomous negotiation (Avoma, 11 Mar).
No economic buyer is mapped; the initiative came down from unnamed procurement leadership Zycus has never met, so there is no one funded to say yes (Avoma, 11 Mar).
The buyer capped urgency himself, saying New Balance is far away from autonomous capability and needs process maturity first, which points at a no-decision outcome (Avoma, 11 Mar).

deal_momentum.text: No buyer-side activity since 11 Mar; the promised leadership feedback never came and only rep-side health-check calls were logged on 12 May.
The 21 May stage move to Formal Evaluation was an internal edit by the rep, not a buyer milestone, so it overstates real progress.
Earlier momentum was real: RFI submitted 06 Mar, acknowledged by Damiano 09 Mar, and a full demo delivered 11 Mar.

customer_commitment.text: Damiano committed only to pass findings to leadership and be in touch, then never did, so there is no forward commitment on record.
No dates, no next meeting requested, and no Zycus-specific next step were secured on 11 Mar.

deal_risk.text: Status-quo inertia is the primary risk: Ariba stays for P2P, the org is not ready, and doing nothing is the default path (Avoma, 11 Mar).
Single-thread and no economic buyer mean one silent contact can stall the deal indefinitely, which is exactly what has happened for ~117 days.

customer_commitment.evidence: No forward commitment; promised feedback never returned; dark ~117 days.

deal_momentum.evidence: No buyer activity since 11 Mar; only rep-side health tasks and internal edits.

deal_risk.evidence: Do-nothing inertia, no EB, single-thread, org self-described as far from ready.

customer_preference.evidence: Positive product read ('easier to use and more robust than what we have today', 11 Mar) but no decisive lean and no Zycus-specific next step; he deferred entirely to leadership and went silent.

business_case.evidence: Clear qualitative pain (ease of use, supplier friction, scaling, manual negotiation) but no numbers; Damiano said 'it's tough to say quantitative right now' (11 Mar).

expansion_context.note: Net-new business (Type set to New Business 03 Feb 2026); no sibling Closed-Won on New Balance, so executive and stakeholder access is NOT inherited and the unmapped economic buyer is a genuine early-stage gap.

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: activities last 120d; field history full; contact roles + tasks reviewed
gaps: Economic buyer / budget owner unnamed: evaluation sponsored by 'procurement leadership' and a global indirect sourcing director, but no named contact in Salesforce.
Deal single-threaded: Damiano Grillo is the only buyer contact across all calls, tasks, and contact roles.
No buyer engagement since 11 Mar 2026 (~117 days); latest evidence is four months old, lowering recency confidence.
